findings.field: Professional Summary / bullet 1; Professional Summary / bullet 2; DiDi Experience / bullet 5
findings.issue: JD-relevant tech/process terms are not consistently bolded inć­Łć–‡. `Agile` is left plain, and `Spark` is only implicit in `Spark-oriented` rather than being surfaced as a bold tech token.
findings.fix: Bold `Agile` wherever it appears inć­Łć–‡, and write `**Spark**` explicitly when referring to Spark-oriented analytics workflows.

findings.issue: The lead framing is transition-first (`transitioning from data analytics`) rather than role-first, so the first scan reads as a career change before capability.
findings.fix: Open with the target direction first, e.g. backend/software-engineering or security-focused delivery, then mention the data-analytics background as supporting context.

findings.issue: The category title `APIs` is too vague for a first-pass read and looks like a bucket label rather than an informative skill group.
findings.fix: Rename it to a more specific label such as `Integration`, `Engineering Practices`, or `Delivery`.

findings.issue: JD must-have `Agile` is not listed in Skills, so ATS may miss an exact keyword match even though the term appears in body text.
findings.fix: Add `Agile` explicitly to the most relevant Skills line and keep the existing body evidence.

findings.issue: The resume does not yet explain why an analytics-to-engineering path is relevant to HealthEquity's healthcare-consumer domain, so the team-direction bridge is weak.
findings.fix: Add one bridge clause about auditability, regulated data handling, or consumer-impacting internal workflows.

findings.issue: The DiDi section reads like sustained daily use of many programming stacks in one role (`C#`, `Python`, `JavaScript`, `Scala` plus `SQL`/`HTML`/`CSS`), which can feel overstated unless qualified as primary vs adjacent work.
findings.fix: Separate the primary implementation stack from supporting analytics/frontend work, or explicitly label the adjacent stacks as partnered/integrated rather than daily owned.

revision_priority: [MUST] Add `Agile` explicitly to Skills so the JD match is exact and ATS-safe.
[MUST] Reframe the Summary opening to be role-first, not transition-first, and add one direct bridge to regulated or consumer-impacting workflows.
[SHOULD] Qualify the DiDi stack breadth so it reads as primary ownership plus adjacent collaboration, not five-plus daily languages.
[NICE] Rename the `APIs` Skills bucket to a more informative label.
raw_json.revision_instructions: Rewrite the Summary opener to lead with the target role direction first, then mention the data-analytics background as supporting context. Add `Agile` explicitly to Skills; if you want stricter ATS matching, also surface `Spark` as an exact token rather than only inside `Spark SQL` or `Spark-oriented`. Add one short bridge phrase tying the work to regulated, auditable, consumer-facing workflows relevant to HealthEquity. If keeping the DiDi section as-is, qualify the language breadth so the primary stack is clear and the adjacent stacks read as partnered integration work rather than daily ownership.

revision_priority: [MUST] Add `Agile` explicitly to Skills so the JD match is exact and ATS-safe.
[MUST] Reframe the Summary opening to be role-first, not transition-first, and add one direct bridge to regulated or consumer-impacting workflows.
[SHOULD] Qualify the DiDi stack breadth so it reads as primary ownership plus adjacent collaboration, not five-plus daily languages.
[NICE] Rename the `APIs` Skills bucket to a more informative label.
